name: Acebo from the Jevero natural pool — circular through Jálama
name_original: Acebo desde la Piscina natural Jevero — circular por Jálama
region_intro: Mountain biking around Acebo, nestled in the Sierra de Gata mountain range in Cáceres, Spain, offers diverse terrain for riders. The region is characterized by its mountainous geography, with elevations reaching up to 1,492 meters at Jálama Mountain. Riders can expect routes that traverse riverside forests, extensive pine forests, and undulating relief, often following paths parallel to streams and forest tracks. The landscape features numerous natural pools and viewpoints, providing varied scenery for mountain bike trails. Best mountain bike trails around Acebo The most popular mountain bike trail is Trevejo desde Hoyos — circular por la Sierra del Gata, a 16.3 miles (26.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 50 minutes to complete. This route explores the Sierra del Gata with varied forest and track sections. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Acebo from the Jevero natural pool — circular through Jálama, a difficult 31.7 miles (51.0 km) path. This challenging route features significant elevation gain and passes by the Jevero natural pool and through the Jálama area. Local mountain bikers also love the Sierra de Gata: Jálama Loop, a 46.5 miles (74.9 km) trail leading through the mountainous Sierra de Gata, often completed in about 7 hours 25 minutes. Mountain biking around Acebo is defined by mountainous geography, natural pools, and extensive forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult, catering to various riding skills and fitness levels. The routes in Acebo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. 71 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Acebo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Acebo?
A: The Acebo area, within the Sierra de Gata, offers a dedicated mountain biking infrastructure with over a dozen routes listed on komoot. The broader Sierra de Gata region boasts an International Mountain Bicycling Association (IMBA) approved MTB center, featuring 10 distinct routes that span a total of 233 kilometers of signposted paths.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Acebo?
A: Mountain biking in Acebo offers a varied landscape, from smooth paths to rocky sections and singletrack. You'll find routes traversing riverside forests, extensive pine forests, and undulating terrain, often following paths parallel to lively streams and forest tracks. The region's mountainous geography ensures a dynamic riding experience.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails suitable for intermediate riders?
A: Yes, there are several moderate trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1252115935/la-ermita-de-virgen-de-la-pena-desde-cilleros-circular-por-la-sierra-de-santa-olalla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Ermita de Virgen de la peña desde Cilleros - circular por la Sierra de Santa Olalla&lt;/a&gt; route is a moderate 23.9 km ride with a duration of about 1 hour 43 minutes, offering a good challenge without being overly technical.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while mountain biking near Acebo?
A: The area is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter refreshing natural pools like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4840158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riofrío Natural Pool&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5993164" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Pools of the Jevero River&lt;/a&gt;. The trails also offer views of significant peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4958182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Jálama Summit&lt;/a&gt;, the second-highest mountain in Sierra de Gata.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or panoramic spots along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain around Acebo provides several opportunities for panoramic views. While specific viewpoints aren't always marked on every trail, the region is known for its 'Miradores' (viewpoints), such as Mirador de la Ventosa, offering expansive vistas of the Sierra del Jálama and surrounding reservoirs. Many routes will naturally offer scenic overlooks due to the undulating relief.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Acebo?
A: The Sierra de Gata region, including Acebo, is generally pleasant for mountain biking during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be hot, though the natural pools offer a refreshing break. Winters are typically mild, but higher elevations might experience cooler conditions. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes starting near Acebo?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are designed as circular loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1250953529/trevejo-desde-hoyos-circular-por-la-sierra-del-gata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trevejo desde Hoyos — circular por la Sierra del Gata&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular trail exploring the Sierra del Gata. Another option is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251009325/acebo-from-the-jevero-natural-pool-circular-through-jalama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acebo from the Jevero natural pool — circular through Jálama&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Acebo?
A: The mountain bike trails around Acebo are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 5.0 stars. Riders often praise the varied terrain, from riverside and pine forests to challenging mountainous sections, and the opportunity to explore the natural beauty of the Sierra de Gata.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks accessible by mountain bike?
A: Beyond natural features, the region offers glimpses into rural culture. You might pass through charming villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6050936" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Martín de Trevejo&lt;/a&gt;, known for its traditional architecture. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4821468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Almenara&lt;/a&gt;, a historic tower, is another point of interest in the wider area.

Q: Are there any particularly long or challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a significant challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1252070202/sierra-de-gata-jalama-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Gata: Jálama Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 74.9 km trail that typically takes around 7 hours 25 minutes to complete. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19432679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo De Trevejo – San Martín de Trevejo loop from Cilleros&lt;/a&gt;, covering 72.6 km.

Q: Can I find trails that pass by natural pools for a refreshing break?
A: Absolutely. Acebo is famous for its natural pools. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251009325/acebo-from-the-jevero-natural-pool-circular-through-jalama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acebo from the Jevero natural pool — circular through Jálama&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, directly passes by the Jevero natural pool, offering a perfect spot to cool down during your ride.
tips: Walk the ascent to Alto de la Navera (Jálama) 1485 masl, to have an incredible view of the entire Sierra de Gata region and its magnificent landscape.

There is something better than jumping into the river on a warm summer day after a walk. The natural pools of the Jevero (Acebo) River are the perfect answer. With on-site parking, you can enjoy starting your adventure here and resting up afterward.

waypoints.name: Alto de la Navera (Jálama) Summit and Lookout Tower
Natural Pools of the Jevero River (Acebo)
